title: dryopteris lilloi
intro: Dryopteris lilloi, also known as Lillo's wood fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, native to the Andean region of South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health issues. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. In traditional herbal medicine, Dryopteris lilloi is prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
